title: Statistics (Full Elite 30Q)
subject: math
summary: Statistics focuses on interpreting data, averages, spread, and distributions.
SPTG frequently tests mean, weighted average, median, and simple interpretation.
Harder problems involve algebra inside averages, combined groups, and variance concepts.
coreConcepts: Mean (average) = total sum / number of items.
Median is the middle value when data is sorted.
Mode is the most frequent value.
Weighted average applies when different groups contribute unequally.
Adding/removing data changes the mean in predictable ways.
Combined mean depends on total sums, not just averaging means.
Variance measures spread around the mean.
Standard deviation is the square root of variance.
SPTG often hides algebra inside average problems.
derive: If mean = m, then total sum = m × n.
New mean after adding a value = (old sum + new value)/(n+1).
Variance = average of squared deviations from mean.
formulas: New mean after adding x = (old sum + x)/(n+1)
traps: Do not average averages directly unless group sizes are equal.
Mean changes with adding/removing values — always recompute total.
Median depends on order, not values directly.
Outliers can heavily affect mean but not median.
Range depends only on extremes, not all data.
Weighted average problems often hide weights in words.
If total changes, always track sum, not just mean.

sptgSet.question: The mean of 5 numbers is 10. Find their total sum.
The mean of 4 numbers is 6. A new number 10 is added. Find new mean.
Find the mean of first 10 natural numbers.
The mean of 6 numbers is 12. One number is removed and new mean is 10. Find removed number.
Find mean if sum = 120 and n = 8.

hardSet.question: Mean of 10 numbers is 15. Two numbers 10 and 20 are removed. Find new mean.
Mean of 8 numbers is 12. One number 20 replaced by x. New mean is 13. Find x.
Combined mean of group A (5 students, mean 10) and B (5 students, mean 20).
Combined mean: group A (4, mean 10), group B (6, mean 20).
Find mean deviation conceptually if all values equal.
If all values increase by 5, how does mean change?
If values are multiplied by 2, how does mean change?
Find variance of identical values set.
Mean of 5 numbers is 8. Add x to all values. New mean = 10. Find x.
If mean is 20 and one value increases by 10, what happens to mean (n=5)?

pat1Set.question: Find mean of first n natural numbers.
If mean = 10, variance = 9, find std dev.
Weighted mean with different group sizes.
If variance increases, what happens to spread?
Median vs mean when outlier exists.
Find mean after removing extreme value.
